    9. Too Many Cooks In The Kitchen Of Your Mind
</h2>

<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p>Having too many mentors is just an elaborate method of procrastination.</p><p>When you outsource your thinking... Then you don't have to take action. That way you can keep searching for some "ultimate truth" that you're looking for. Convincing yourself that maybe it's that next super-wise person that's gonna give it to you.</p><p>Truth is more elusive than that.</p><p>You will never believe anything that you don't physically experience yourself.</p><p>Instead... Once you get your information from a credible source... Act on it. Right away.</p><p>See, you can hear the same thing from 8 different people in 8 different ways in 8 different languages and it will never truly sink in. Not until you go out and experience it yourself. In your bones. Make it your flesh. Right now, if you're struggling with moving forward it may be that there are too many cooks in the kitchen of your mind.</p><p>You know when you hear those magic words come out of someone's mouth and you're like wow... that felt like a breakthrough?</p><p>Don't go and ponder it.</p><p>Go act on it. Immediately.</p><p>Get another coating of plasma on your brain to etch that emotion memory into your noggin for good. That's the only way to make it truly sink in.</p><p>You're looking for a certain spirit. And once you find that spirit, that feeling, it doesn't need corroberation. This isn't science. If it works once.... If it brings you higher... Then why do you need to hear any one else say it for?</p><p>But here you are again. Unable to act. Despite the fact that you have a million mentors. Why? Because you have a million mentors. Having so many inputs buys you time so you can "think" about it. Then, after you think about it, you get a second opinion, right? that's why you read all those books and why you listen to all those podcasts.</p><p>And then when somewhere along the way you get a contradictory opinion... Everything stops. Then you're confused. Someone has to be lying. You have this internal debate. Which person is right? Which concept is true? It's literally "over-thinkers anonymous".</p><p>When all you have to do in the first place is go out there and test it for yourself.</p><p>You don't need to take anyone's word for it.</p><p>The people who are further along the journey... All they can do for you, is point to the holes in the ground that THEY came across.</p><p>But who knows, maybe those holes already got covered up? Or maybe there are other holes those people didn't see. You gotta go out there and discover things for yourself. Don't get me wrong... Those further along than you... They can help you. By making you pay attention to things you may have otherwise missed. But what they CAN'T do is do the pushups for you. </p><p>What you may be suffering from is called Information Junkie Syndrome.</p><p>You know what I'm talking about, right? It's when you say:</p><p>Let me go watch a YouTube video about meeting strangers. Let me read about how to promote myself. Let me read about making better art. Anything to avoid the thing you should be doing. </p><p>That's how you end up standing still. </p><p>You can't get your information from a million different sources and then expect it all to be cohesive. That's not how it works.</p><p>Successful people indeed have mentors. But ONE at a time.</p><p>You need to invest... in one system.</p><p>It's possible to take opposite approaches and arrive at the same results.</p><p>Like, you can tell someone that they have to meditate and approach things "with love" in order to find their success. And that may be absolutely true for them in that moment.</p><p>Or, you can tell someone that they need to tap into their anger and focus by putting pictures of their haters on their fridge to find their success... And, that, may be true also.</p><p>It's not the system that gets you there.</p><p>It's the fact that you're only following ONE system them that gets you there. </p><p>Focus.</p><p>And then once you've moved ahead on the board game. Once you get to a point you're satisfied with. Then... Only then... You can change systems if you gotta.</p><p>Go window shopping for new mentors if you need to.</p><p>Do whatever you gotta do. But for now only use one at a time so that when you use the framework you've chosen... it's like a silver bullet.</p><p>Stop being afraid of missing out on something.</p><p>Stop using information as a drug that pacifies you from actually going out there and taking action.</p><p>Follow one mentor.</p><p>Find that person that doesn't have just the words, but that also captures a certain spirit about them that can lead you into action.</p><p>Words are only half of it. Mentors will use them to tire you out, until all you have left to do is ACT... </p><p>That's why someone that's doing it, or being it already, is the perfect mentor.</p><p>Because when they shut up... They're still speaking the truth.</p><p>Pick THAT person to be your Mr. Miyagi for the next little while.</p><p>And do whatever they say. Even if you don't understand why you're "waxing on, waxing off".</p><p>Once you've found that person or that system... Seriously... ignore everything else.</p><p>That is the sort of dedication that gets results.</p><p>#messagewave</p><p></p>
</div>
